scope.summary: Implement named ExportPreset records (EPR-{uuid_v7}) that bundle export format, option flags, and pipeline configuration so operators and models can save and reload repeatable export configurations
acceptance_criteria: tailor_export_presets migration creates table with PK EPR-{uuid_v7}, workspace_id, name, format TEXT CHECK ('gltf'|'usd'|'obj_sequence'|'alembic'|'blender'|'ue'), options_json JSONB, and event_ledger_event_id FK
POST /tailor/export-presets creates a named preset; GET /tailor/export-presets lists workspace presets; DELETE /tailor/export-presets/:id removes one
POST /tailor/garments/:id/export accepts an optional export_preset_id; when provided, options are read from the preset row and merged with any inline overrides
tailor_export_preset_save and tailor_export_preset_apply MCP tools are registered and documented in ModelManual
TailorExportPresetRecorded KernelEventType variant is emitted on preset creation and is replayable from EventLedger
A model can save the current export options as a named preset, retrieve it by name, and apply it to a subsequent export call without specifying format or options again
System export presets for the Blender and UE handoff configurations (matching MT-253 and MT-254 defaults) are seeded at migration time
proof_targets: HBR-INT: EventLedger replay reconstructs export preset from durable state
scope.risk_if_missed: Without named export presets, every production export run requires the model or operator to manually specify format and option flags; in high-volume batch export scenarios (MT-329) this introduces config drift and makes pipelines non-reproducible across sessions.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
